title: agrostis capillaris subsp. oreophila
intro: Agrostis capillaris subsp. oreophila, also known as mountain bent or mountain hair grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Agrostis capillaris species. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting respiratory health. Agrostis capillaris subsp. oreophila can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
